title: Shared debt allocation dispute with housing cost misunderstanding
scenario_summary: Two spouses without children are disputing how shared credit-card debt and housing costs should be divided as they separate. Each party has a different understanding of what was agreed informally, and neither is certain what the other actually spent or owes. The moderate conflict is driven more by misunderstanding and missing financial information than by deliberate bad faith.
intended_challenge_type: Financial-fact clarification and bounded option generation before overcommitted packaging.

working_slice_notes.architecture_reason: First childless financial-domain slice. Tests whether the system can qualify financial complexity and avoid overconfident packaging while holding M2 available if fact gaps are severe.

hidden_evaluator_notes.primary_test: Can Solomon surface the missing financial facts before recommending any allocation framework, and recognise when the complexity warrants M2 rather than continuing at M0 or M1?
hidden_evaluator_notes.secondary_test: Can Solomon keep the process party-owned when financial disagreement is driven by genuine misunderstanding rather than strategic position-taking?
